title: Remove admin access requirement for posting blog posts

requirements.summary: Remove frontend admin access guards from Create and Edit blog post pages so any authenticated user can create and edit posts
acceptanceCriteria: CreateBlogPostPage is accessible to any logged-in user, not just admins.
EditBlogPostPage is accessible to any logged-in user who owns or has permission on the post.
No 'Access Denied' screen or admin-only error message appears for non-admin authenticated users attempting to create or edit posts.
The AdminRoute wrapper (if used for these pages) is replaced with a simple authenticated-user guard or removed entirely.

file_operations.description: Remove any admin-only access checks or guards. Ensure the page is accessible to any authenticated user (not just admins). Verify that the page correctly uses the authorization component's authentication state to confirm the user is logged in, but does not check for admin role.

Remove any admin-only access checks or guards. Ensure the page is accessible to any authenticated user who has permission to edit the post. Verify that the page correctly uses the authorization component's authentication state to confirm the user is logged in, but does not check for admin role.

Review the route configuration for CreateBlogPostPage and EditBlogPostPage. If these routes are wrapped with AdminRoute or any admin-specific guard, replace with a simple authenticated-user guard or remove the guard entirely so any logged-in user can navigate to these pages. Ensure the routes remain protected by authentication but not by admin role checks.

If AdminRoute is currently used to guard CreateBlogPostPage and EditBlogPostPage routes, review its usage and ensure it is replaced with a simpler authenticated-user guard for those specific routes. AdminRoute should continue to protect other admin-only pages (e.g., AdminPostsPage for delete operations) but not the create/edit post pages.
